rules.name: List top-level property names
rules.description: Uses the 'keys' operator to return all top-level property names of a JSON object. Useful for discovering which properties are set on an object.

rules.name: List top-level property values
rules.description: Uses the 'values' operator to return all top-level property values of a JSON object.

rules.name: List distinct visual types on a page
rules.description: Uses the 'distinct' operator to remove duplicates from an array. Here it returns the unique set of visual types used across all visuals on a page.

rules.name: Check that a property is of a specific type
rules.description: Uses the 'typeof' operator to return the JSON type of a value as a string: 'string', 'number', 'boolean', 'array', 'object', or 'null'. Useful for validating schema expectations.

rules.name: Check that a required property exists
rules.description: Uses the 'hasprop' operator to check if a JSON object contains a given property key. Unlike 'var', this correctly distinguishes between 'property is null' and 'property does not exist'.

rules.name: Split a delimited string into parts
rules.description: Uses the 'strsplit' operator to split a string by a delimiter and return an array. Useful for parsing dot-separated paths, comma-separated lists, etc.

rules.name: Join array elements into a string
rules.description: Uses the 'strjoin' operator to concatenate array elements with a separator. Here it joins visual names with a comma.

rules.name: Extract substrings matching a regex pattern
rules.description: Uses the 'regexextract' operator to find all matches of a regex pattern in a string. Returns an array of matched strings. Optionally specify a capture group index as the third parameter.

rules.name: Use first non-null value as fallback
rules.description: Uses the 'coalesce' operator to return the first non-null value from a list. Unlike 'or', coalesce does not skip falsy values like 0, false, or empty string - it only skips null.

rules.name: Get first N items from an array
rules.description: Uses the 'slice' operator to extract a sub-array. Follows JavaScript Array.prototype.slice() semantics: slice(array, start, end). Supports negative indices to count from the end.

rules.name: Get last 2 items from an array
rules.description: Uses the 'slice' operator with a negative start index to get the last N items from an array.

rules.name: Get current UTC timestamp
rules.description: Uses the 'now' operator with no parameters to return the current UTC date/time as an ISO 8601 string.

rules.name: Get a relative date (e.g. 7 days ago)
rules.description: Uses the 'now' operator with an offset and unit to compute a relative date. Supported units: seconds, minutes, hours, days, months, years. A negative offset means 'before now'.

rules.name: Calculate difference between two dates in days
rules.description: Uses the 'datediff' operator to compute date2 - date1 in specified units. Default unit is 'days'. Supported units: seconds, minutes, hours, days. A positive result means date2 is after date1.

rules.name: Calculate difference between two dates in hours
rules.description: Uses the 'datediff' operator with an explicit 'hours' unit.

rules.name: Check that a visual config has required properties
rules.description: Combines 'keys', 'hasprop', and 'filter' to find visuals missing a required config property. Returns names of visuals that don't have the expected property.

rules.name: Check that page does not exceed N distinct visual types
rules.description: Combines 'distinct' with 'count' to limit the number of different visual types used on a single page.

rules.name: Ensure a property is a string after coalesce
rules.description: Combines 'coalesce' and 'typeof' to verify the resolved value is of the expected type.
